sections.label: 1. Call the regular meeting to order.
2. Pledge of Allegiance.
3. Presentations and Proclamations.
a. Presentation of a proclamation acknowledging February 23 - 27, 2026 as National Invasive Species Awareness Week in the City of Fairfax.
b. Presentation of a proclamation acknowledging February 27 - March 8, 2026 as Winter 2026 Fairfax City Restaurant Week in the City of Fairfax.
c. Presentation by the City Manager on the proposed FY27 Operating and CIP Budgets.
4. Adoption of the agenda.

5. Initial general public comment period (A maximum of three (3) minutes per speaker for any city matters not requiring a public hearing. The speakers who have registered with the city clerk’s office (703-385-7935) prior to 5:00 p.m. the day of the m
7. Appointments to Boards, Commissions, and Advisory Committees.
11. Recess the regular meeting to go into a work session.
12. Work Session.
a. Presentation by Police and Human Services: A Coordinated Community Response.
b. Update and discussion of the Citywide Level of Service Study, which will assess impacts and cost implications of new development on City services and infrastructure.
c. Pre-application briefing for a potential multifamily residential development located at 10340 Democracy Lane.
d. Pre-application briefing for a potential redevelopment of the Courthouse Plaza Shopping Center at 10300 Willard Way.
13. Reconvene the regular meeting.
14. Future Meeting Topics
15. City Manager report out (if needed).
16. Council Comments and Committee reports out (if needed).
18. Adjournment.
